title: androsace nivalis subsp. nivalis
intro: Androsace nivalis subsp. nivalis, also known as snow anemone, is a small, alpine perennial plant belonging to the Primulaceae family. It is a low-growing, white-flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, where it grows in rocky areas and alpine meadows. The medicinal properties of Androsace nivalis subsp. nivalis include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, with the leaves and flowers being used to treat respiratory issues and the roots being used to treat digestive problems.
